subject: [PATCH] mm: make mmap_miss accounting symmetric for VM_SEQ_READ

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about the patch not being symmetric for VM_SEQ_READ mappings and agreed to restructure in v2, but did not provide any specific details on how the restructuring would address the issue.
reviews.raw_body:
The patch is a result of review from sashiko on another one of my patches:
https://lore.kernel.org/all/8edc8cd0-f65c-4456-9b3f-362e744c9a96@linux.dev/

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch makes the mmap_miss accounting symmetric for VM_SEQ_READ mappings by skipping the decrement in both do_async_mmap_readahead and filemap_map_pages when the mapping is sequential read. This ensures that the counter only moves for mappings that also participate in the increment side, preventing a single mapping from biasing the counter for other mappings of the same file.

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch makes the mmap_miss accounting symmetric for VM_SEQ_READ mappings by skipping the decrement in both do_async_mmap_readahead and filemap_map_pages when the mapping is sequential read. This ensures that the counter only moves for mappings that also participate in the increment side, preventing a single mapping from biasing the counter for other mappings of the same file.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch description does not explicitly mention adding Reviewed-by tag, and suggested adding it.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch makes the mmap_miss accounting symmetric for VM_SEQ_READ mappings by skipping the decrement in both do_async_mmap_readahead and filemap_map_pages when the mapping is sequential read. This ensures that the counter only moves for mappings that also participate in the increment side, preventing a single mapping from biasing the counter for other mappings of the same file.
